mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand as the cost of capital rises. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Cumulative 60-day SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of periods with sustained PAYEMS growth above 150k, or Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.08.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand. When both series decline month-over-month simultaneously, equity valuations compress as investors anticipate lower corporate revenues. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month in same month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of co-contraction events, or joint interaction term has t-statistic < 1.5 in multivariate regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below its 3-month moving average, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflationary pressures. When this occurs alongside elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 315), equity returns compress as profit margins face dual pressure from weak demand and rising input costs. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 3-month MA and CPIAUCSL > 315
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of stagflation trigger events, or the interaction coefficient between INDPRO decline and CPIAUCSL elevation is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration. This triggers a negative equity repricing as investors anticipate reduced consumer spending and lower corporate earnings. S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 54% of unemployment spike events, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and forward returns is below 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter remains positive and above 2% annualized rate, indicating sustained economic expansion. This baseline growth signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in continued earnings growth and capital deployment opportunities.
failure_condition: Cumulative 60-day SPX returns are negative in more than 48% of quarters with positive GDP growth above 2% annualized, or correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward returns is below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below its 6-month trend while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, the real interest rate rises, compressing equity valuations. This regime-specific mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices terminal rate expectations upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 56% of real tightening events, or the interaction term between inflation deceleration and elevated rates has t-statistic < 1.3 in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above their 6-month moving average while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow month-over-month, signaling synchronized demand expansion across residential and consumer sectors. This joint demand acceleration produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices cyclical growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 6-month MA and RSAFS grows month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 52% of synchronized demand acceleration events, or the joint interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in multivariate analysis over rolling 2-year windows.
